target.title: fix(provider): route thinking profiles by model API
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes custom Anthropic-compatible providers whose selected model API is `anthropic-messages` but whose provider id is not literally `anthropic`.
- Carries the selected catalog/config model API into thinking policy resolution so Anthropic Messages routes can expose `adaptive`/`max` levels instead of falling back to generic/off behavior.
- Keeps owner precedence intact: direct bundled provider policy still wins, exact runtime provider hooks can override API-family fallback, and API-family bundled policy only runs after provider-owned hooks decline.
- Out of scope: changing provider auth, model catalog contents, config shape, migrations, or transport behavior.

## Linked context

Closes #91975

Related: the issue's ClawSweeper review identified the missing API fact in thinking-policy dispatch and recommended reusing API-owner routing without provider-id substring matching.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: Custom providers with `api: "anthropic-messages"` now route thinking profile lookup through the Anthropic policy fallback after exact provider hooks decline, so Claude models expose Anthropic thinking levels instead of silently losing extended thinking controls.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw CLI/source checkout on Node 24, using the PR branch at commit `3b413143dbc6f4a64149e00106403aa651e6933a` with dependencies installed via `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm openclaw --version`; then `node --import tsx --input-type=module` with OpenClaw's `resolveProviderThinkingProfile` runtime resolver for provider `jdcloud-anthropic`, model `claude-sonnet-4-6`, and API `anthropic-messages`.
Evidence after fix: Copied console output from the local OpenClaw source checkout:

```text
$ pnpm openclaw --version
OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (3b41314)

$ node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'END_PROOF_SCRIPT'
import { resolveProviderThinkingProfile } from './src/plugins/provider-thinking.ts';

const provider = 'jdcloud-anthropic';
const modelId = 'claude-sonnet-4-6';
const api = 'anthropic-messages';
const profile = resolveProviderThinkingProfile({
  provider,
  context: {
    provider,
    api,
    modelId,
    params: {},
    reasoning: undefined,
    compat: undefined,
  },
});

console.log(`openclaw_source_commit=${process.env.OPENCLAW_PROOF_COMMIT ?? '3b413143dbc6f4a64149e00106403aa651e6933a'}`);
console.log(`provider=${provider}`);
console.log(`model=${modelId}`);
console.log(`api=${api}`);
console.log(`thinkingProfile=${profile ? 'resolved' : 'missing'}`);
console.log(`thinkingLevels=${profile?.levels.map((level) => level.id).join(',') ?? 'none'}`);
console.log(`thinkingDefault=${profile?.defaultLevel ?? 'none'}`);
END_PROOF_SCRIPT
openclaw_source_commit=3b413143dbc6f4a64149e00106403aa651e6933a
provider=jdcloud-anthropic
model=claude-sonnet-4-6
api=anthropic-messages
thinkingProfile=resolved
thinkingLevels=off,minimal,low,medium,high,adaptive,max
thinkingDefault=adaptive
```

Observed result after fix: The real OpenClaw resolver returns a resolved Anthropic thinking profile for the custom `jdcloud-anthropic` provider because the selected model API is `anthropic-messages`; the exposed levels include `adaptive` and `max`, and the default is `adaptive`.
What was not tested: No live external Anthropic/JDCloud API request was made; this change is local provider thinking-policy/profile resolution, not network transport or auth.
Proof limitations or environment constraints: The proof uses the local OpenClaw source runtime resolver and CLI version output rather than a remote provider account; focused tests, lint, build, and autoreview are listed below as supplemental validation.
Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The regression tests added in this PR encode the prior failure mode where the selected model API was available in catalog/config but was not passed into provider thinking-policy dispatch.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/thinking.test.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/provider-public-artifacts.test.ts extensions/anthropic/provider-policy-api.test.ts`
- `pnpm format:check -- extensions/anthropic/provider-policy-api.test.ts extensions/anthropic/provider-policy-api.ts src/auto-reply/thinking.test.ts src/auto-reply/thinking.ts src/plugins/provider-public-artifacts.test.ts src/plugins/provider-public-artifacts.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.ts src/plugins/provider-thinking.ts src/plugins/provider-thinking.types.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/anthropic/provider-policy-api.test.ts extensions/anthropic/provider-policy-api.ts src/auto-reply/thinking.test.ts src/auto-reply/thinking.ts src/plugins/provider-public-artifacts.test.ts src/plugins/provider-public-artifacts.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.ts src/plugins/provider-thinking.ts src/plugins/provider-thinking.types.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm build`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

Regression coverage added for API propagation, bundled policy lookup by manifest model-catalog API refs, Anthropic Messages custom-provider profiles, model-level API override precedence, runtime hook precedence over API fallback, and runtime fallback when an unrelated API-ref bundled surface declines.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? Yes

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No

Highest-risk area: Provider thinking-policy ownership and fallback ordering.

Risk mitigation: Direct bundled policy remains first for literal bundled providers, runtime provider hooks win before API-family fallback, and common/non-unique API refs that decline no longer block runtime provider hooks.

## Current review state

Next action: Waiting for the refreshed Real behavior proof check, CI, and ClawSweeper review.

Still waiting on: Maintainer review and any broader CI/Testbox signal maintainers want beyond the focused local proof above.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: Local autoreview initially found three actionable issues around config-derived API context, model-level API overrides, and API fallback precedence; all were fixed and the final autoreview run was clean.
